---
title: "Preset Options Smoke Test"
subtitle: "One template, layered options, local overrides"
author:
  - name: "First Author"
    affiliation: "Department, Institution"
brand: _brand.yml
format:
  academic-poster-typst:
    poster-preset: methods-heavy
    poster-style: minimal
    poster-size: 36x24in
    margin:
      x: 0.35in
      y: 0.3in
    poster-spacing:
      header-padding:
        x: 0.32in
        y: 0.18in
    footer-center: "Preset fixture"
execute:
  echo: false
---

::: {.poster-column}

# Question {.compact}

Can a poster start from a named preset while still keeping local edits readable in YAML?

# Approach {.compact}

- `poster-preset: methods-heavy` selects a two-column, compact base.
- `poster-style: minimal` swaps the visual treatment.
- `poster-spacing.header-padding` checks nested YAML overrides.

:::

::: {.poster-column}

# Result {.compact .secondary}

The document-level options stay short, and the Markdown body remains ordinary poster content.

::: {.poster-callout .light}
Local YAML wins after the preset bundle.
:::

# Next Step {.compact}

Use this file as a quick smoke test when changing preset resolution or Typst merge logic.

:::
